channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Sourcery vs GitHub Copilot: Review vs Generation
item.description: Quick Verdict Sourcery and GitHub Copilot are not really competing for the same buyer. Sourcery is a specialist - a dedicated AI code review and refactoring platform that focuses entirely on...

item.title: How to Connect AI Agents to Enterprise Productivity Tools Securely (2026 Architecture Guide)
item.description: Most enterprise AI agents today can analyze but can't execute. They summarize documents, surface insights, and draft responses. They don't close support tickets, update Salesforce, or trigger...

item.title: The easiest way to make butterfly animation without writing a single line of CSS or JS! 🚀
item.description: Want to make your elements fly like a real butterfly? 🌸 Forget about long class names or messy JavaScript. We just launched the most elegant update for Butterfly CSS. 🦄 What’s the Big Deal? Most...

item.title: System Design From Scratch: The Components That Actually Run Production Systems
item.description: You open amazon.com. A product page loads in under a second. Behind that single page load, your request hit a DNS server, bounced through a CDN edge node, passed a rate limiter, got distributed by...

item.title: Go Bitwise Flags and Bitmasks: Configuration Pattern Guide
item.description: I was adding my eighth boolean toggle to a config struct — EnableCompression, SkipValidation, LogQueries, and so on. Eight fields, eight if cfg.Field checks, eight more lines in my YAML. The...

item.title: How I Actually Use Claude as a Senior Dev Partner (Not Just a Code Generator)
item.description: Most developers I know use Claude the same way: paste some code, ask a question, get an answer, move on. It works. But it's leaving most of the value on the table. After a few months of treating...

item.title: How to Add Comments to an Astro Site (Lazy-Loaded, Zero JS by Default)
item.description: Astro's "ship zero JavaScript by default" model is a perfect fit for comment widgets — they're the one thing on the page that actually needs to be interactive, and they're the perfect candidate...

item.title: Building a Full Player Onboarding Flow in UE5 (Account Email Character Creation)
item.description: Dev Log #5 — Full New Player Flow (Magickness™) We’re currently in the foundation phase of development for Magickness™, focusing on connecting core systems into a working player experience. This...

item.title: I Built a 5-Line SDK for Claude Agents — Here's Why
item.description: Every team building Claude agents writes the same 300 lines of glue code. I got tired of it and built claude-runner — a thin TypeScript wrapper that turns the official Agent SDK into a 5-line...

item.title: Meet Trysil: a lightweight ORM for Delphi
item.description: If you have ever written a Delphi application that talks to a database, you know the routine: write SQL by hand, manage parameters, loop through datasets, and copy values into objects field by...

item.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
item.description: ChatGPT had reportedly been used to plan the attack that killed two and injured five at Florida State University last April. The family of one victim has said that they plan to sue OpenAI over the...

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II mission to fly around the far side of the Moon
item.description: Humans haven’t set foot on the Moon since NASA’s Apollo 17 mission in 1972. Now, the space agency is racing to get back to the lunar surface under the umbrella of its Artemis program — a nod to...

item.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
item.description: After falling victim to a hacker, Mercor is facing lawsuits and reportedly losing big-name customers.

item.title: Instagram now lets you edit your own comments
item.description: If you make a mistake in an Instagram comment, you can now finally fix it without having to delete the comment and make it again. On Thursday, Instagram announced that users can now edit their...

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: The app was ranking No. 57 on the App Store just before Meta AI's new model launched. Now it's No. 5 — and rising.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Anthropic said this week that it limited the release of its newest model, dubbed Mythos, because it is too capable of finding security exploits in software relied upon by users around the world....

item.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
item.description: The two tech giants are looking to co-develop custom chips, at a time when demand for CPUs is high due to a growing global shortage.

item.title: Netflix’s TV games get a big boost with Jackbox collection
item.description: Netflix's party-focused lineup of TV games just got three excellent new additions: Jackbox's Drawful 2, Fibbage 4, and Quiplash 3, available as the Jackbox Party Essentials pack on the platform....

item.title: Framework is teasing a lot of Linux for its April 21st event
item.description: Modular PC maker Framework is hosting a "Next Gen" event on April 21st at 1:30PM ET, and it looks like it might have something to do with Linux. Alongside a newsletter announcing the event,...

item.title: The 70-Person AI Image Startup Taking on Silicon Valley's Giants
item.description: Black Forest Labs has long punched above its weight in the AI image generation space. Its next move? Powering physical AI.

item.title: Google’s Gemini AI can answer your questions with 3D models and simulations
item.description: Google's latest upgrade for Gemini will allow the chatbot to generate interactive 3D models and simulations in response to your questions. With the new feature, you may see options to rotate the...

item.title: EngageLab SDK Flaw Exposed 50M Android Users, Including 30M Crypto Wallets
item.description: Details have emerged about a now-patched security vulnerability in a widely used third-party Android software development kit (SDK) called EngageLab SDK that could have put millions of...

item.title: Sierra’s Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over
item.description: Last month, Sierra launched Ghostwriter, an agent designed to build other agents. With this “agent as a service” tool, the startup intends to replace traditional click-based web applications with...

item.title: Castlevania headlines a big list of exciting indie game reveals
item.description: A big indie showcase has just wrapped up, and now I have a lot more games to look forward to. The third iteration of the Triple-I Initiative, an annual showcase for upcoming releases put on by a...

item.title: The team behind 1000xResist is making a game about convincing an AI that it isn’t human
item.description: When the team at indie studio Sunset Visitor were wrapping up their critically acclaimed first game, 1000xResist, it wasn't long before they started thinking about what they'd tackle next....

item.title: UAT-10362 Targets Taiwanese NGOs with LucidRook Malware in Spear-Phishing Campaigns
item.description: A previously undocumented threat cluster dubbed UAT-10362 has been attributed to spear-phishing campaigns targeting Taiwanese non-governmental organizations (NGOs) and suspected universities to...

item.title: Mastodon is about to launch its take on Bluesky’s starter packs
item.description: Mastodon is preparing to roll out "Collections" in the next few weeks, a feature that allows you to find and create lists of accounts worth following, according to an announcement on Thursday....

item.title: Slay the Princess’ developer is the latest to expand into indie game publishing
item.description: Black Tabby Games, the two-person studio behind the hit indie games Slay the Princess and Scarlet Hollow, is getting into indie publishing. The publishing arm, which was revealed on Thursday, is...

item.title: The MacBook Neo is the best thing to happen to Windows in years
item.description: If there's one thing I know about Microsoft after covering the company for more than 20 years, it's that it will always respond to a competitive threat. Apple's MacBook Air convinced Microsoft and...

item.title: Amazon CEO takes aim at Nvidia, Intel, Starlink, more in annual shareholder letter
item.description: Andy Jassy's annual shareholder letter reads something like a diss track to a wide range of competitors as he defends spending $200 billion in capex.
